issues.title: Planner, registry, and router disagree on executable tools

evidence.detail: Planner emits send_email, calendar_create_event, home_assistant_call_service, weather_now, time_now, and web_search.
Registry registers time_now, weather_now, web_search, send_email, and home_assistant.
execute_tool hard-codes supported_tools to time_now, weather_now, and web_search, with weather_now and web_search returning not implemented.

Direct execute_tool checks returned real output for time_now, not implemented for weather_now/web_search, and unknown tool errors for send_email/calendar_create_event/home_assistant_call_service when policy was bypassed.

issues.suggested_fix: Create one authoritative executable tool catalog, make Planner use only that catalog, implement real handlers for any documented tools that remain exposed, and add integration tests proving every planner-emitted tool runs end to end through execute_tool.

issues.title: Docker build context can capture secrets and local state

evidence.detail: Does not exclude .env, .venv, venv, config, data, tests, or most local caches.
Runtime stage copies the full repo with COPY . .
This workspace contains a local .env and gitignored runtime state, so a normal docker build would include workstation-specific content unless excluded.

issues.suggested_fix: Replace broad COPY operations with an allowlist of runtime files, expand .dockerignore to exclude secrets, virtualenvs, caches, docs, tests, and local runtime data, and document expected runtime mounts explicitly.

issues.title: Dependency and packaging artifacts disagree on the supported runtime matrix

evidence.detail: CPU-only file pins torch==2.7.1+cu118.
Installs torch==2.7.1, torchvision==0.20.1, and torchaudio==2.5.1, which does not match the other manifests.
Optional ml dependencies allow torch>=2.6.0,<2.9.0, torchvision>=0.17.0,<0.23.0, torchaudio>=2.6.0,<2.9.0.
scripts/validate_deployment.py still expects torch 2.5.x and failed the environment in this workspace.

issues.suggested_fix: Define a canonical supported install matrix for base, CPU, cu118, and cu124, then sync pyproject extras, requirements files, Dockerfile, Pipfile notes, and validation scripts to that matrix.

issues.title: Core quality gates are not green

evidence.detail: ruff check rex reported 339 errors.
black --check rex would reformat 37 files.
mypy rex found 272 errors in 53 files.

issues.suggested_fix: Run a staged cleanup for import order, unused code, formatting, and structural typing in the core package, then enforce the resulting baseline in CI at least for touched files.

issues.title: Repo-integrity tests are brittle and fail on pre-existing dirtiness

evidence.detail: Fails whenever git status shows tracked modifications, regardless of whether the tests caused them.
Uses the same clean-worktree assumption.
Main pytest run ended with exactly two failures, both caused by the pre-existing dirty tracked file requirements-gpu-cu124.txt.

issues.suggested_fix: Capture a git-status baseline at test start and compare against that baseline, or scope the check to files modified during the test session rather than the whole worktree state.

issues.title: Security audit script produces high false-positive noise

evidence.detail: Current scan behavior does not exclude .mypy_cache and similar generated directories.
scripts/security_audit.py scanned 10398 files and reported 295 placeholder findings, including many from .mypy_cache.

issues.suggested_fix: Restrict the scan to tracked source files or a curated source allowlist, exclude generated caches and virtualenvs, and separate source findings from documentation findings.

issues.title: Deployment validation script is stale and contradicts the current repo

evidence.detail: Validation logic still expects older torch and env-driven runtime assumptions.
scripts/validate_deployment.py exited non-zero with Score: 5/7 checks passed.
The script flagged missing REX_ACTIVE_USER and expected torch 2.5.x even though the repo now centers JSON runtime config and different torch versions.

issues.suggested_fix: Rewrite the deployment validator to check the current JSON runtime config model, current torch ranges, and current entrypoints instead of stale env-var and version assumptions.

issues.title: Runtime configuration documentation is inconsistent with the code

evidence.detail: Describes .env as secrets-only and directs runtime settings to config/rex_config.json.
Still presents large env-var configuration sections and an env-variable-centric GUI story.
Still instructs users to configure runtime behavior via environment variables.
Warns that legacy non-secret env vars are ignored at runtime.

issues.suggested_fix: Standardize all setup and runtime docs around one configuration model, keeping .env for secrets only unless a setting is truly env-backed, and update GUI wording to match.

issues.title: Windows quickstart and startup instructions are wrong

evidence.detail: Says python rex_assistant.py starts a wake-word assistant and tells users to configure via environment variables.
Running python rex_assistant.py showed a text chat loop: Rex assistant ready. Type 'exit' or 'quit' to stop.

issues.suggested_fix: Rewrite the Windows guide to distinguish text chat, voice loop, dashboard, and TTS API entrypoints, and update the config instructions to the current JSON-plus-secrets model.

issues.title: Architecture, release, and status documents are stale or false

evidence.detail: Claims rex/voice_loop.py is a compatibility wrapper to rex/voice_loop_optimized.py.
Targets master.
Targets main.
Claims the project is production-ready despite current lint/type/deployment results.

issues.suggested_fix: Archive or relabel stale status reports, update architecture docs to match the current code, and make branch/release documentation and automation agree on one branch strategy.

issues.title: Voice loop and root-level entry surfaces are duplicated and drifting

evidence.detail: Contains a full voice-loop implementation rather than a thin wrapper.
Also contains a full implementation.
Presents itself as the canonical implementation.
mypy rex surfaced multiple voice-loop-related type issues, reinforcing that this surface is already drifting.

issues.suggested_fix: Pick one canonical voice-loop implementation, reduce the others to thin wrappers or remove them, and update docs/tests to point to the single maintained path.

issues.title: Audit replay is explicitly stubbed

evidence.detail: Replay reconstructs a ToolCall and returns a placeholder result instead of executing the tool.

issues.suggested_fix: Either implement dry-run replay through the existing tool router with clear side-effect rules or remove replay from active capability claims until it is real.

issues.title: Scheduler workflow triggering is explicitly stubbed

evidence.detail: The module notes that workflow triggering is stubbed and logs would trigger workflow for workflow_id jobs.

issues.suggested_fix: Wire scheduled workflow jobs into WorkflowRunner or remove the workflow_id scheduling story from the exposed feature surface until it is implemented.

issues.title: Repo root mixes product code, legacy wrappers, reports, and operational artifacts

evidence.detail: Root inventory showed entrypoints, wrappers, logs, reports, helper scripts, and historical notes all in the top-level directory.
A major active runtime surface lives at the repo root instead of inside the canonical package.
Historical status reporting is also stored at the repo root alongside runtime code.

issues.suggested_fix: Move historical reports to a reports/archive area, consolidate scripts, keep only deliberate entrypoints at the root, and clearly mark compatibility wrappers.
